user.title: Belgian Water Prices
user.description: Enter your postcode and we'll match you to the right water utility. If we can't, you'll pick from a list on the next step.

manual.title: Pick your water utility
manual.description: We could not resolve a utility for that postcode. Pick yours below.

options.title: Annual consumption
options.description: Your annual water consumption in cubic metres. Used by the projected-cost sensor; defaults to 80 m³/year. Flemish utilities also need the number of registered residents (drives the basis volume) and an opt-in for the VMM social tariff. Optionally point at a water meter sensor (cumulative m³, device_class=water) so the year-to-date cost sensor can read consumption since 1 January from Home Assistant's recorder.

data.commune: Commune (per-commune utilities only: De Watergroep / Farys / Water-link)
data.water_meter_sensor: Water meter sensor (optional override; auto-detected from the Energy dashboard if blank)

abort.already_configured: This water utility is already configured.

data.commune: Commune (per-commune utilities only: De Watergroep / Farys / Water-link)
data.water_meter_sensor: Water meter sensor (optional override; auto-detected from the Energy dashboard if blank)

snapshot_stale.title: {utility} tariff snapshot is stale
snapshot_stale.description:
The last successfully fetched {utility} tariff is {age_days} days old (valid until {valid_until}). The integration is still serving the cached numbers, but they may no longer match the published tariff. Last fetch error: {last_error}.

This usually clears on its own once the utility republishes the page or the network issue resolves. If it persists, check the integration's GitHub issues or the daily live-check workflow run.
